hierarchy.name: Easy TeSys Motor Control System
hierarchy.description: Schneider Electric Easy TeSys product family for industrial motor starting, protection, and control up to 690V.

hierarchy.name: 3-Pole Contactor Assembly
hierarchy.description: DPE-series 3-pole contactors (9A–38A, AC-3) for switching and controlling 3-phase motor loads.

hierarchy.name: Thermal Overload Relay Assembly
hierarchy.description: DPER-series Class 10 thermal overload relays (0.1A–32A) providing motor overload protection with phase-loss sensitivity.

hierarchy.name: Manual Motor Controller
hierarchy.description: GP2E-series manual motor controllers with integrated thermal and magnetic protection, suitable for isolation per IEC 60947.

hierarchy.name: Power Contacts (3-Pole)
hierarchy.description: Three main power switching contacts rated 9–38A (AC-3), 690V max operational voltage, 1 million electrical durability cycles.

hierarchy.name: Electromagnetic Coil
hierarchy.description: AC (24–240V) or DC (24V) excitation coil that generates the magnetic force to close the power contacts. Inrush 70VA, sealed 7VA.

hierarchy.name: Auxiliary Contact (Integrated)
hierarchy.description: 1 NO or 1 NC instantaneous auxiliary contact rated 10A, 690V, 30 million mechanical cycles. Provides status signaling.

hierarchy.name: Auxiliary Contact Block (DPEAN11)
hierarchy.description: Front-mount clip-on auxiliary contact block providing 1 NO + 1 NC contacts, rated 10A/690V, 30 million mechanical cycles.

hierarchy.name: Thermal Sensing Element (Bimetallic)
hierarchy.description: Bimetallic current-sensing element with temperature compensation (-20 to +60°C) that trips at 1.14 ± 0.06 × Ir.

hierarchy.name: Trip Mechanism
hierarchy.description: Mechanical trip mechanism actuated by the bimetallic element, with manual reset capability and phase-loss detection.

hierarchy.name: Overload Relay Auxiliary Contacts
hierarchy.description: Auxiliary contacts providing trip status signaling, IP2X protection, conforming to IEC 60947-5-1.

hierarchy.name: Manual Switch Mechanism
hierarchy.description: Rotary or toggle switch mechanism providing ON/OFF/TRIP positions, suitable for isolation per IEC 60947-1 §7-1-6.

hierarchy.name: Thermal Trip Unit
hierarchy.description: Adjustable thermal overload trip element (0.1–32A setting range), Class 10 tripping characteristic.

hierarchy.name: Magnetic Trip Unit
hierarchy.description: Instantaneous magnetic short-circuit trip element with fixed trip current (Id ±20%), providing short-circuit protection.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Fails to conduct current through the power contacts
failure_modes.end_effect: Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Conducts current at insufficient capacity due to degraded contact surface
failure_modes.end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Intermittently conducts current through the power contacts
failure_modes.end_effect: Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Uncommanded conducting of current through power contacts
failure_modes.end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Conducts current too late after coil energization command
failure_modes.end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Fails to cease conducting current when coil is de-energized
failure_modes.end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Erratically conducts current with unstable contact resistance
failure_modes.end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Fails to generate magnetic force to close the contactor

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Generates insufficient magnetic force to fully seat the armature
failure_modes.end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Intermittently generates magnetic force due to coil connection fault
failure_modes.end_effect: Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Uncommanded coil generates magnetic force without control signal
failure_modes.end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Generates magnetic force too late after voltage is applied to coil
failure_modes.end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Fails to cease generate magnetic force when supply is removed
failure_modes.end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

failure_modes.syntactical_description: Erratically generates magnetic force causing armature chatter
failure_modes.end_effect: Nuisance user experience due to non-hazardous heat, vibration, or elevated noise levels.
